name: Luzón - Alcolea del Pinar – circular por el río Tajuña y Camino del Cid
region_intro: Mountain biking around Luzón offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills and river valleys. The terrain includes paths alongside the Tajuña River and through forested areas, providing diverse riding experiences. Riders can expect moderate climbs and descents across the region's varied natural features. The area provides a mix of natural trails and established paths suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Luzón The most popular mountain bike trail is Luzón - Alcolea del Pinar – circular por el río Tajuña y Camino del Cid, a 33.6 miles (54.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route follows the Tajuña River and parts of the Camino del Cid. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Peña Rubia – Anguita Village and Church loop from Anguita, a moderate 25.6 miles (41.1 km) path. This loop explores the area around Anguita, offering views of the village and surrounding countryside. Local mountain bikers also love the Maranchón loop from Maranchón, a 15.8 miles (25.4 km) trail leading through the local terrain around Maranchón, often completed in about 2 hours 17 minutes. Mountain biking around Luzón is defined by river valleys, open countryside, and moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, with a focus on moderate routes. The routes in Luzón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Luzón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Luzón?
A: There are over 10 mountain bike trails around Luzón, offering a variety of routes through river valleys and open countryside. Most of these routes are considered moderate in difficulty, with one easy option available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Luzón?
A: Mountain biking around Luzón is characterized by routes through river valleys, open countryside, and forested areas. You can expect moderate elevation changes, with paths alongside the Tajuña River and through varied natural features.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Luzón?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail in Luzón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Tajuña River Gorge loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 26.8 km route that takes approximately 1 hour 53 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Luzón?
A: The trails in Luzón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, including paths along the Tajuña River and through forested areas, offering engaging experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Luzón?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Luzón are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005419371/luzon-alcolea-del-pinar-circular-por-el-rio-tajuna-y-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luzón - Alcolea del Pinar – circular por el río Tajuña y Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; is a 54 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31510274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón loop from Maranchón&lt;/a&gt; is a 25.4 km circular trail.

Q: What are some interesting places to see along the mountain bike trails in Luzón?
A: While exploring the trails, you can discover several interesting places. Consider visiting the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4272783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4341854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro de Luzón&lt;/a&gt;, or the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5040946" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, Luzón offers several natural attractions. You can find the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5999892" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Judes&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4320256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajuña River Gorges&lt;/a&gt;, and the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6144074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maranchón Juniper Forest&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454951" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valley of Miracles&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de los Casares&lt;/a&gt; are also notable natural monuments.

Q: What is a good moderate trail for mountain biking in Luzón?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Rubia – Anguita Village and Church loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt;. This 41.1 km path offers views of the village and surrounding countryside, typically taking around 3 hours 6 minutes to complete.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails in Luzón typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Luzón varies depending on the route and your pace. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005419371/luzon-alcolea-del-pinar-circular-por-el-rio-tajuna-y-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luzón - Alcolea del Pinar – circular por el río Tajuña y Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; takes about 4 hours 13 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Río Tajuña Forests loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; is around 3 hours.

Q: Are there any trails that explore the Tajuña River area specifically?
A: Yes, several trails follow or cross the Tajuña River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005419371/luzon-alcolea-del-pinar-circular-por-el-rio-tajuna-y-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luzón - Alcolea del Pinar – circular por el río Tajuña y Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; explicitly follows the Tajuña River, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Anguita Village and Church – Río Tajuña Forests loop from Anguita&lt;/a&gt; also explores forests near the river.
tips: This picturesque and quiet town on the banks of the Tajuña River is usually an obligatory stop for those who travel the well-known cycling routes of the Camino del Cid or Montañas Vacías that cross wonderful landscapes.

The gorges of the Tajuña River form a landscape of great beauty between the towns of Anguita and Luzón. Along about 11 kilometres, the passage of the river has created curious cracks and caves in the rock. If you are doing the Camino del Cid, it is worth deviating from the road and venturing down a dirt track to enjoy this environment.
This remote spot is located on the banks of the Tajuña River and surprises with its curious red wine-colored rock formations that blend perfectly with the surrounding landscape, worthy of a postcard.

Undoubtedly one of the main tourist attractions in Alcolea del Pinar, this stone house is also a work of art in itself. Its builder, Lino Bueno, took more than 20 years to excavate the sandstone using only hand tools. Today it is a museum that is worth visiting, an ideal place if you are here with children.

waypoints.name: Church of San Pedro de Luzón
